children.name: Rhea
children.description: Large flightless birds native to South America, superficially resembling ostriches but smaller and adapted to temperate grasslands and open woodlands. Both species are gregarious and polygynous.

children.name: Rhea americana
children.description: The largest South American bird, standing up to 1.5 metres tall and weighing up to 40 kilograms. It inhabits the grasslands, pampas and open scrub of eastern Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay and Argentina. Males incubate eggs and raise chicks from multiple females in a shared nest.

children.name: Rhea pennata
children.description: Also known as Darwin's Rhea, this smaller species is found in the high-altitude Altiplano of the Andes and the windswept steppes of Patagonia. Its mottled brown plumage provides excellent camouflage against the arid landscape. It is a swift runner and a strong swimmer when crossing rivers.
